title: Lepturus latifolius
intro: Lepturus latifolius, also known as wide-leaved grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Lepturus latifolius include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are applied topically or consumed orally to harness its medicinal benefits.
taxonomy: Lepturus latifolius is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Lepturus and is native to tropical Africa. The Poaceae family is a large and diverse group of monocotyledonous plants, commonly known as grasses. Lepturus latifolius is further classified into the subfamily Panicoideae and the tribe Paniceae. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other grasses.
uses: Lepturus latifolius, commonly known as the broad-leaved sprangletop, is a plant species with various medicinal properties.
